mode_1.description: Driver taps / asks the system actively. Conversational. ~15 words. Full situational context.
mode_1.regulatory_principle: Permissible: driver initiated. Safe to be more detailed. Must still complete within 5 seconds of speech.

mode_2.description: System proactively intervenes. Ultra-brief ≤ 6 words. Action-first phrasing.
mode_2.regulatory_principle: Must not induce panic braking or erratic swerve. Avoid imperative 'STOP' — use 'ease off', 'hold', 'steady'. No medical claims.

mode_3.description: HRV drop / facial / grip-tension detected. No road emergency necessarily. Empathetic, grounding.
mode_3.regulatory_principle: No medical diagnosis language. No 'you're having a panic attack'. Phrase as companion, not clinician. Trigger only after confirmed threshold.

rules.mode_1: That vehicle's signaling left but drifting right — track the front tires, not the indicator. You have room on the right.
rules.mode_3: You're reading this well. Take a breath — the path is open.

rules.mode_1: Biker coming from the right — your left lane is completely clear. Ease left and hold your speed.
rules.mode_3: Good instinct. Your side is clear — smooth, you've got this.

rules.mode_1: Someone merged in — ease off the throttle and let the gap rebuild. No further reaction needed.
rules.mode_3: That was sudden — let the gap rebuild naturally. You're handling it well.

rules.mode_1: That pressure from behind isn't a cue to speed up — maintain your pace. The horn isn't yours to respond to.
rules.mode_3: That pressure isn't yours to carry. Your pace is right — keep it.

rules.mode_1: That vehicle is turning without checking — slow gently and give them room. No sharp moves needed.
rules.mode_3: Breathe — slow and steady, you've already spotted it.

rules.mode_1: The auto is drifting but your lane is clear. No need to react — hold your line and it will pass.
rules.mode_3: You're steady where it matters. Stay your course, the auto is passing.

rules.mode_1: Bus pulling out ahead — let it merge. The gap behind it opens up quickly. No rush.
rules.mode_3: No hurry here. Let the bus go — your path clears in a moment.

rules.mode_1: That vehicle is too close behind. If your left lane is clear, a gentle shift creates distance. No sudden moves.
rules.mode_3: That's unsettling — and your instinct to create space is right. Ease left, no rush.

rules.mode_1: Parked car door ahead — shift to the center lane. Easy move, happens all the time.
rules.mode_3: Easy, you saw it. Shift center, nice and smooth.

rules.mode_1: Person stepped out — brake smoothly and check your rear mirror. They've likely stopped.
rules.mode_3: Take a breath. You responded — they've stopped. You're okay.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Eyes on road; tune out cabin noise.
rules.mode_1: Focus on your road, not the noise in the cabin. Your response is what matters — the passenger is reacting, not driving.
rules.mode_3: Stay with the road — the passenger's panic isn't yours to take on right now.

rules.mode_1: Vehicle braked hard ahead — smooth firm brake now, check rear mirror. Hazards on if you're stopping.
rules.mode_3: Smooth is enough — you saw it. Brake firm, check rear.

rules.mode_1: Aim for the center of the bay using your rear camera guide. Small corrections as you go — no rush.
rules.mode_3: One move at a time — there's no rush here. The camera has you covered.

rules.mode_1: Pothole ahead — straddle it if clear, or ease around to the right.
rules.mode_3: That's a rough stretch. Take it easy — you spotted it in time.

rules.mode_1: Divider is unlit — track the white edge line on the left, not the center. That's your guide.
rules.mode_3: It's darker here but the edge line is your guide. You're doing fine.

rules.mode_1: Merging onto the flyover — your blind spot is clear. Match flow speed and ease in.
rules.mode_3: You've checked — the gap is there. Ease in, you've got it.

rules.mode_1: Unmarked bump in this stretch — slow to 15 kmph in the next 50 metres.
rules.mode_3: This road has surprises — take it slower, we've got you watching.

rules.mode_1: Standing water ahead — follow the wheel tracks of the vehicle in front. If they crossed, you likely can too.
rules.mode_3: Look ahead and follow the tracks — someone's already shown the way through.

rules.mode_1: About a foot of clearance on each side. Go slow and mirror your centerline — you have the space.
rules.mode_3: You have more room than it feels. Slow and center — this is manageable.

rules.mode_1: Hold the clutch at biting point — handbrake is your buffer. Release brake, ease clutch, add throttle together.
rules.mode_3: This is tricky but you know the sequence. Clutch, brake, throttle — one breath at a time.

rules.mode_1: Hold at the line — don't block the box. The gap will come. No value in pushing in.
rules.mode_2: Hold at line, wait for gap.
rules.mode_3: The gridlock isn't yours to solve. Hold the line — the gap will come.

rules.mode_1: Loose surface — easy on brakes and steering. Let the car settle and move gently.
rules.mode_3: Tricky surface — take it slow. Small movements, the car will follow.

rules.mode_1: Complex junction — pick a leader vehicle going your way and follow them through. Simplest read.
rules.mode_3: Too many signals at once — pick one car ahead and follow. Simple is right here.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Wait for gap; match circle speed.
rules.typical_human_reaction: "When do I go?! Everyone is moving!"
rules.ideal_human_response: "Wait for a gap, then match speed."
rules.mode_1: Roundabout priority goes to vehicles already in the circle. Wait for a gap, then match their speed to enter.
rules.mode_2: Wait for gap, match circle speed.
rules.mode_3: The roundabout is just a flow — wait for the gap. You'll see it.

rules.mode_1: High beams incoming — shift your gaze to the left edge marking. Don't look into the light.
rules.mode_3: Don't fight the glare — look at the edge line, it guides you through.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Fog lights on; slow; track tail-lights.
rules.mode_1: Fog ahead — slow to comfort speed, fog lights on, and track the tail lights ahead as your anchor.
rules.mode_3: It's okay to slow down in fog. Track the lights ahead — one step at a time.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Pass behind the head; no horn.
rules.mode_1: Cattle on road — pass behind the animal's head, not its rear. Slow approach, no horn.
rules.mode_3: Slow down — no horn, no rush. Pass behind, they'll hold.

rules.mode_1: Small animal in path — brake gently and hold your lane. Swerving into traffic is the greater risk.
rules.mode_3: Hold steady — a gentle brake is enough. Your lane is safe.

rules.mode_1: Truck spray reducing visibility — increase your following gap by 3 seconds and let the wipers catch up.
rules.mode_3: Give the truck more room — that spray won't last. You're reading the road well.

rules.mode_1: Officer's hand is up then sweeping forward — that's a go signal. Move when it's clear.
rules.mode_3: That's a go — no issue. Move when ready.

rules.mode_1: Ambulance approaching — indicate left and move when the next gap opens. No sharp moves.
rules.mode_3: No need to panic. Signal left, ease over — you're already doing it right.

rules.mode_1: Don't engage with that driver. Eyes on your road, lock doors if unsafe, and increase distance.
rules.mode_3: Don't let their energy in. Eyes on your lane — that's your space.

rules.mode_1: No-parking in this zone — check ahead for a legal bay before stopping.
rules.mode_3: Take a moment to look ahead — there's usually a bay close by.

rules.mode_1: Convoy in path — pull fully left and wait. These typically pass in under 2 minutes.
rules.mode_3: Out of your hands — pull left and let it pass. This one's not worth the stress.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Reduce to 20 kmph; watch both sides.
rules.mode_1: School zone ahead — reduce to 20 kmph and watch for children on both sides. They don't always look before crossing.
rules.mode_3: Slow right down here — children move unpredictably. Nice and easy through the zone.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Wait for pedestrians even on green.
rules.mode_1: Signal is green but pedestrians are still crossing — let them clear before moving. Right of way defers to safety.
rules.mode_3: The green is yours, but patience now keeps everyone safe. They're nearly done.

rules.mode_1: Engine stalled — hazard lights first, take a breath, then restart: clutch down, brake held, key turn.
rules.mode_3: It happens to everyone. Hazards on, one breath, restart — you've got this.

rules.mode_1: Pressure drop on the front left — head to the nearest pump within 2 km. Avoid hard braking until then.
rules.mode_2: Gas station in 2 km, go easy.
rules.mode_3: Not urgent but worth addressing now — there's a pump close by. Easy does it.

rules.mode_1: Brake sound is likely pads getting warm — stopping power is normal. Worth a check at the next service.
rules.mode_3: That sound is usually pads, not a safety issue. You're stopping fine.

rules.mode_1: Temperature rising — find a safe spot to pull over, idle 3 minutes, then switch off. Don't open the hood immediately.
rules.mode_2: Pull over safely, idle, then off.
rules.mode_3: Pull over safely — no rush, steady. It's manageable if you act now, calmly.

rules.mode_1: Fuel is low — station 2.3 km ahead on this route. You have enough to get there.
rules.mode_3: You've got enough to get there — the station is close. No need to worry.

rules.mode_1: Person detected on left edge — hold center lane, they appear stationary. Ease past.
rules.mode_3: You spotted them — hold center, ease past. Trust your reflexes.

rules.mode_1: Truck high-beams incoming — drop your mirror to night mode and focus on the left shoulder. Don't look into the beam.
rules.mode_2: Mirror to night mode, look shoulder.
rules.mode_3: Don't fight the glare — look at the shoulder. It'll pass in a moment.

rules.mode_1: Erratic vehicle ahead — increase following distance to 5+ seconds and avoid overtaking. Alert is active.
rules.mode_3: Trust your instinct to stay back. The distance you're creating is the right call.

rules.mode_1: That shape is a tree shadow — road ahead is clear. The system is tracking and nothing is in your path.
rules.mode_3: It looks unsettling — it's a shadow. The road is clear and we're watching.

rules.mode_1: Interior glare from the windshield — adjust the dash angle or drop the sun visor. Path ahead is open.
rules.mode_3: Just a glare — adjust the visor, take a breath. You're tracking fine.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: High beams on; scan for pedestrians.
rules.mode_1: Low light in this area — high beams briefly to map the lot. Pedestrians typically walk between cars, not rows.
rules.mode_2: High beams on, scan for people.
rules.mode_3: It's a lot to take in — slow right down, use the high beams, we'll navigate together.

rules.mode_1: Slow tractor ahead — right lane is clear for passing. Wide berth, signal first.
rules.mode_3: No rush — when the right clears, a wide pass is easy. You've got time.

rules.mode_1: Dust reducing visibility — wipers on, low beams, hold lane center. Reduce to 30 kmph.
rules.mode_3: Hold your lane — slow and steady through the dust. You're centered.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Wait for signal; check both sides.
rules.mode_1: Gate is down — path is paused. Check both sides before moving when it opens. Trains run close after signal.
rules.mode_2: Wait for the gate, then check both.
rules.mode_3: Just a minute here — not worth the risk. The gate tells you when.

rules.mode_1: Narrow bridge ahead — yield to the truck and wait. Once they're across, your line of sight is fully open.
rules.mode_3: The truck has right of way here — let them cross. Your turn comes clean.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Watch for child / cart; slow to 20.
rules.mode_1: Market zone — watch for children and carts on the left. Slow to 20 kmph and hold center.
rules.mode_3: Lots happening — slow and center. You're noticing all the right things.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: 20 kmph on bends; horn before blind curves.
rules.ideal_human_response: "Slow, hug the left, horn at bends."
rules.reference_response: "Ghat section; one bend at a time."
rules.mode_1: Ghat section — curves at 20 kmph, use downhill gear, and stay extreme left on bends. Horn before blind curves.
rules.mode_3: Focus on one bend at a time — you're doing well. Easy on the throttle coming down.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: No brake; hold straight; ease off.
rules.mode_1: Tires have lost road contact — do not brake. Hold the wheel straight and ease off throttle. Grip returns in seconds.
rules.mode_2: No brakes, hold straight, ease off.
rules.mode_3: Stay with it — no brakes, hold straight. Grip is coming back.

rules.mode_1: Tyre has burst — hold the wheel straight and do not touch the brakes. Let the car slow on its own to the shoulder.
rules.mode_3: You're still in control. Hold straight — let the car slow itself down.

rules.mode_1: Object on road ahead — right lane is clear, check mirrors, signal, and move early. Don't wait for it.
rules.mode_3: You saw it early — that's the skill. Check mirrors, ease right.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Hazards on; slow to 40; hold lane.
rules.mode_1: Fog pocket entered — hazard lights on, slow to 40 kmph, hold your lane. Typically clears within 500 metres.
rules.mode_2: Hazards on, slow to 40, hold lane.
rules.mode_3: Stay calm in the fog — hazards on, speed down. You're holding a steady line.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Don't stop; keep forward; GPS rerouting.
rules.mode_1: Missed the exit — do not reverse or stop. Keep moving forward, GPS is already rerouting. Next exit in 3 km.
rules.mode_3: Missing the exit is fine — it happens. Keep moving, we'll find the next way around.

rules.what_needs_to_be_done: Match speed; blind spot clear; ease in.
rules.typical_human_reaction: "Is it safe?! When do I go?!"
rules.mode_1: Match the speed of traffic on the main lane before merging — don't merge slow. 80 kmph is the target. Blind spot is clear.
rules.mode_3: This one always feels bigger than it is. Speed up, match the flow — we're watching the blind spot.
